item.title: Logic for Programmers v0.15, Livecoding 摘要
item.description:
Logic for Programmers version 0.15 has been released as the first release candidate, with all content finalized and copy-edited. The next release will be version 1.0 in print. The author also recommends two livecoding music languages: Strudel, a JavaScript DSL ported from Haskell's TidalCycles, and CLAVIER-36, a 2D grid-based language inspired by Orca. These were demonstrated at the inaugural livecode.chicago meetup.
标签: programming, logic, book, livecoding, music, JavaScript, DSL, open-source, creative-coding, release

item.title: Quoting Charity Majors 摘要
item.description:
In 2025, the economics of code production were fundamentally inverted: generating code became effectively free and instant, rather than hard, time-consuming, and expensive. This shift transformed code from a treasured, carefully curated asset into a disposable and regenerable commodity. Charity Majors argues that this new reality demands more engineering discipline, not less, as the ease of code generation introduces new challenges in quality, maintenance, and system design.
标签: AI, software engineering, code generation, economics of software, engineering discipline, AI-assisted programming, generative AI, LLMs, Charity Majors

item.title: You Got Faster. Your Company Didn’t. 摘要
item.description:
The article argues that while AI makes individual workers more productive, it can slow down the entire organization. Using AI to generate long, unedited documents shifts the cognitive burden from the writer to the readers, who must fact-check and understand the AI-generated content. This creates a 'Ponzi scheme' where time is transferred rather than saved. The author advocates for editing AI output and maintaining the implicit contract that writers should compress and verify information to save readers' time. The piece concludes that individual speed gains don't translate to team efficiency if the work quality degrades.
标签: AI, productivity, workplace, communication, writing, teamwork, efficiency, documentation, collaboration, management

item.title: Pluralistic: The (real) dead economy theory (17 Jun 2026) 摘要
item.description:
Cory Doctorow critiques the 'dead economy theory,' arguing that financial markets have detached from productive value, exemplified by Elon Musk's net worth rising to $1 trillion despite a string of flops since 2020. He contrasts this with the real risk of AI: not that AI will replace jobs, but that executives will replace workers with defective chatbots. Doctorow warns that capital is being diverted from real research (e.g., cancer) into bubbles like crypto, AI, and the SpaceX IPO, creating an economy that destroys value and lives.
标签: economics, dead economy theory, bubbles, crypto, AI, Elon Musk, SpaceX, financial markets, tech criticism, bubble economy
